subject: Gaming
title: Artificial Scarcity and Urgency
description: A quick, practical guide to Artificial Scarcity and Urgency.

hook.text: Limited edition. Only available this weekend. Hurry. These are manufactured pressure tactics, not real scarcity.

buildup.text: Digital items have zero production cost. Making them 'rare' is a psychological trick, not a supply problem.

discovery.text: Scarcity increases perceived value. A skin available for 48 hours feels more valuable than a permanent one.

twist.text: Many 'limited' items return later anyway. The urgency was manufactured. Players bought under false pressure.

climax.text: Artificial scarcity converts browsers into buyers by exploiting loss aversion and FOMO simultaneously.

punchline.text: Zero cost to produce. Infinite copies available. Yet they call it rare.

quiz.question: Why is artificial scarcity a dark pattern in gaming?
options: Digital items have zero production cost, so 'rarity' is manufactured
Because rare items are actually hard to create
